ops.body: Refuter pass complete (criterion da1de59 met): sol xhigh adversarial pass on transition ordering. Verdict — normative order present and D4/D5-core/CAS/settle-barrier properties hold, but 5 properties broken under adversarial scheduling + storage crashes: no fsync anywhere (F1), no already-draining refusal so two distinct-generation Transitions double-own (F2), D2 empty-check/Apply TOCTOU (F3), no cross-journal ownership arbitration (F4), fence-release failure leaks forever (F5); plus AdoptDead deletes the last copy without checking applied count (F6a). Full findings + disposition: daemonkit cc-notes note db838dc. Bounded fixes (F1/F2/F3/F5/F6a/F8 + suite strengthening) queued as a sol hardening lane; F5 policy decided: Fence.Release becomes infallible+idempotent in the interface (consumer panics if it cannot release — crash-on-unexpected, deletes the ignored-error path). F4/F7 are design decisions for the remaining-work plan. P3 stays open until the hardening lane lands and criterion c12cb13's two missing tests are added.
